author: lOrm
content:
VERIFIED-DUP-OF-FIXED (closed via #363 + #440 phase 1). Forensic grep on main @3a19163f:

```
$ grep -n 'READ_TRACKER\|buckets:' src/tools/file/mod.rs
22:/// Maximum number of entries in the read tracker, per session, before
28:/// Per-session bucket: canonical path → monotonic insertion counter.
36:/// Tracks which files have been read, bucketed per session id.
66:pub static READ_TRACKER: LazyLock<ReadFileTracker> = LazyLock::new(ReadFileTracker::new);
73:    buckets: Mutex<HashMap<String, Bucket>>,
110:        let key = super::todo::current_session_key();
150:        let key = super::todo::current_session_key();
```

The Mutex is still process-global but it now guards `HashMap<SessionId, Bucket>`, not a single `Vec<PathBuf>`. Per-session isolation is enforced via `current_session_key()` (see test at line 458, `read_tracker_isolates_marks_between_sessions`). Each session has its own bucket; `mark_read` and `has_been_read` look up by session id, satisfying the core complaint ("session A reading /x lets session B edit /x without reading it" — explicitly tested as false now).

The remaining "global Mutex over the outer HashMap" is briefly held only to fetch/create the per-session bucket, not to scan paths — so the O(N) contention pattern the issue describes is gone. The deeper DIP refactor (inject a `ReadTracker` trait) is the only un-landed sub-point, and #440 description marks it as phase-2 work. Closing as duplicate of #363/#440.
